articles.headline: US Imposes 50% Tariffs on Key Indian Exports, Sparks Trade Dispute
articles.explainer:
• US President Donald Trump announced a 50% tariff on certain Indian exports, effective August 27, 2025.
• The move targets specific sectors where the US alleges unfair trade practices and market access barriers.
• India's Ministry of Commerce expressed strong disappointment, stating the tariffs violate WTO principles and bilateral trade agreements.
• Economic analysts predict potential impacts on India's export-oriented industries and a possible retaliatory response from New Delhi.
• This development escalates trade tensions between the two strategic partners, raising concerns for global supply chains.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: US President Donald Trump announced 50% tariffs on Indian exports.
Tariffs are effective from August 27, 2025.
India's Ministry of Commerce indicated the tariffs violate WTO principles.
This action impacts US-India bilateral trade relations.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of US tariffs on India-US strategic partnership and economic ties.
Discuss India's potential responses, including WTO dispute settlement mechanisms and retaliatory measures.
Evaluate the impact of such protectionist policies on global trade and India's 'Make in India' initiative.
Examine the role of trade disputes in shaping international relations and economic diplomacy.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of US and Indian flags with trade graphs and tariff symbols.
articles.trivia: Historically, trade disputes between major economies often lead to complex negotiations and can sometimes result in new trade agreements.

articles.headline: Rahul Gandhi Alleges Systemic Voter List Fraud by ECI in Connivance with BJP
articles.explainer:
• Congress leader Rahul Gandhi accused the Election Commission of India (ECI) of systemic voter list fraud.
• He alleged this fraud was perpetrated in connivance with the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) across multiple elections.
• Gandhi's claims include discrepancies in electoral rolls, deletion of eligible voters, and fraudulent additions.
• The ECI has yet to issue an official response but has previously stated its commitment to fair and transparent elections.
• This allegation reignites debates on electoral integrity, transparency, and the independence of constitutional bodies.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act
Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
prelimsPoints: Rahul Gandhi (Congress) alleged voter list fraud by ECI and BJP.
Allegations concern systemic discrepancies in electoral rolls.
The Election Commission of India is a constitutional body responsible for conducting elections.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of a robust and transparent electoral process for democratic governance in India.
Examine the powers and functions of the Election Commission of India and challenges to its independence.
Suggest reforms to enhance electoral integrity, including measures for voter list accuracy and grievance redressal.
Analyze the impact of such allegations on public trust in democratic institutions and the electoral system.
articles.imageDescription: Image of a ballot box with a blurred background of a political rally.
articles.trivia: The Election Commission of India was established on January 25, 1950, a day celebrated as National Voters' Day.

articles.headline: Three CRPF Personnel Killed, Ten Injured in Udhampur Gorge Plunge
articles.explainer:
• Three Central Reserve Police Force (CRPF) personnel were killed in an accident in Udhampur, Jammu and Kashmir.
• Their vehicle reportedly plunged into a gorge in the Basantgarh area, causing multiple casualties.
• Ten other personnel sustained injuries and were immediately rushed to a nearby hospital for treatment.
• Authorities have initiated an investigation into the cause of the accident, citing difficult terrain conditions.
• The incident highlights the challenging operational environment faced by security forces in mountainous regions.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention
Security challenges and their management in border areas; linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate
prelimsPoints: Three CRPF personnel died in an accident in Udhampur, J&K.
Their vehicle plunged into a gorge in the Basantgarh area.
CRPF is a Central Armed Police Force (CAPF) under the Ministry of Home Affairs.
mainsPoints: Discuss the operational challenges faced by security forces like CRPF in difficult terrains such as Jammu and Kashmir.
Analyze the welfare measures and risk management protocols for paramilitary personnel in high-risk areas.
Examine the role of infrastructure development and road safety in enhancing the safety of security convoys in border regions.
Highlight the sacrifices made by security personnel in maintaining internal security and territorial integrity.
articles.imageDescription: Image of a CRPF vehicle on a mountainous road in Jammu and Kashmir.
articles.trivia: The CRPF is one of the largest paramilitary forces in the world, primarily responsible for internal security, counter-insurgency, and law and order duties.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5%, Focuses on Inflation Management Amid Global Uncertainties
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to maintain the repo rate at 6.5%.
• This decision aligns with the RBI's ongoing commitment to withdraw accommodation to ensure inflation aligns with the target.
• Governor Shaktikanta Das emphasized vigilance against persistent core inflation and global economic uncertainties.
• The MPC also reaffirmed its stance of 'withdrawal of accommodation' to bring inflation within the 4% target.
• Analysts suggest the RBI is balancing economic growth support with the imperative of price stability.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth
prelimsPoints: RBI's MPC maintained the repo rate at 6.5%.
The stance remains 'withdrawal of accommodation'.
Primary objective is to achieve the inflation target of 4% within a band of +/- 2%.
RBI Governor Shaktikanta Das heads the MPC.
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control and economic growth in the current global economic scenario.
Discuss the effectiveness of monetary policy tools, particularly the repo rate, in managing inflation and liquidity in India.
Evaluate the implications of the 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance on various sectors of the Indian economy, including lending and investment.
Examine the factors contributing to core inflation and the strategies the RBI employs to address them.
articles.imageDescription: Image of the RBI logo with currency notes in the background.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in 2016 and is mandated to meet at least four times a year.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Earth Observation Satellite EOS-08
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched its advanced Earth Observation Satellite (EOS-08).
• The satellite was launched aboard a PSLV-C58 rocket from the Satish Dhawan Space Centre, Sriharikota.
• EOS-08 is designed to provide high-resolution imagery for various applications including agriculture, forestry, disaster management, and urban planning.
• This mission further strengthens India's capabilities in remote sensing and enhances its satellite constellation for environmental monitoring.
• The successful launch underscores ISRO's consistent progress in space technology and its contributions to national development.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights
prelimsPoints: ISRO launched Earth Observation Satellite EOS-08.
The launch vehicle used was PSLV-C58.
The satellite will provide high-resolution imagery for various applications.
Launch took place from Satish Dhawan Space Centre, Sriharikota.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Earth Observation Satellites for India's socio-economic development and national security.
Analyze ISRO's role in advancing India's space capabilities and its contributions to global space exploration.
Evaluate the potential applications of high-resolution satellite imagery in areas like climate change monitoring, disaster management, and resource mapping.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for India in the rapidly evolving global space industry.
articles.imageDescription: Graphic illustration of a PSLV rocket launching an Earth Observation Satellite into orbit.
articles.trivia: PSLV (Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle) is ISRO's third-generation launch vehicle, known for its reliability and versatility.

articles.headline: Government Unveils New National Monsoon Action Plan to Enhance Agricultural Resilience
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare launched a comprehensive 'National Monsoon Action Plan 2025'.
• The plan aims to enhance agricultural resilience against erratic monsoon patterns and climate change impacts.
• Key components include advanced weather forecasting, crop diversification, improved irrigation infrastructure, and farmer awareness programs.
• Focus areas also include drought and flood preparedness, promoting climate-resilient crop varieties, and providing timely advisories.
• This initiative seeks to safeguard food security and stabilize rural incomes amidst changing climatic conditions.

mainsPoints: Discuss the critical role of the monsoon for Indian agriculture and the increasing challenges posed by climate change.

Examine the importance of integrating traditional knowledge with modern technology in developing climate-resilient agricultural practices.
Suggest further policy measures and infrastructural developments required to mitigate the impact of monsoon variability on the agricultural sector.
articles.imageDescription: Farmers working in a field with monsoon clouds in the background.
articles.trivia: India's economy is often described as 'monsoon-dependent' due to the significant impact of rainfall on agricultural output and rural livelihoods.

articles.headline: India-ASEAN Strategic Dialogue Concludes with Focus on Maritime Security and Regional Connectivity
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-ASEAN Strategic Dialogue concluded with a joint declaration emphasizing enhanced cooperation in maritime security.
• Discussions focused on maintaining a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, adhering to international law, including UNCLOS.
• Both sides committed to boosting regional connectivity through physical and digital infrastructure projects.
• Key areas of collaboration include counter-terrorism, cyber security, trade facilitation, and cultural exchanges.
• The dialogue reinforced India's 'Act East Policy' and ASEAN's centrality in the regional architecture.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests

Key focus areas included maritime security and regional connectivity.
Dialogue emphasized a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
UNCLOS (United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea) was reiterated as a guiding principle.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of India's 'Act East Policy' in strengthening ties with ASEAN and its role in the Indo-Pacific region.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for cooperation between India and ASEAN in enhancing maritime security and maintaining regional stability.
Evaluate the potential impact of improved regional connectivity on trade, investment, and cultural exchanges between India and Southeast Asian nations.
Examine how multilateral forums like India-ASEAN dialogue contribute to addressing common regional challenges like terrorism and climate change.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and ASEAN member states arranged together.
articles.trivia: ASEAN was established on August 8, 1967, with the signing of the ASEAN Declaration (Bangkok Declaration) by its five founding members.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Key Provisions of Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India today upheld the constitutional validity of several key provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023.
• The ruling came on a batch of petitions challenging the Act on grounds of fundamental rights violations, particularly privacy and freedom of speech.
• The court emphasized the importance of balancing individual privacy rights with national security and legitimate state interests.
• This judgment provides clarity on data governance in India and sets precedents for future digital regulations.
• The DPDP Act aims to regulate the processing of digital personal data in a manner that recognizes both the right of individuals to protect their personal data and the need to process personal data for lawful purposes.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary

prelimsPoints: Supreme Court upheld key provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023.
The Act aims to regulate digital personal data processing.
The judgment balances privacy rights with national security and state interests.
The Right to Privacy is a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Constitution.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the Supreme Court's judgment on the DPDP Act for India's digital economy and individual privacy rights.
Analyze the challenges in implementing a comprehensive data protection framework in a digitally evolving society like India.
Evaluate the balance struck by the Act between individual privacy, state surveillance, and business innovation.
Examine the implications of this ruling for technology companies, data fiduciaries, and data principals in India.
articles.imageDescription: Gavel and law books with a digital data flow background.
articles.trivia: The Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared the Right to Privacy as a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution.

articles.headline: India's Q1 FY26 GDP Growth Exceeds Projections, Driven by Manufacturing and Services
articles.explainer:
• India's Gross Domestic Product (GDP) growth for the first quarter (April-June) of FY2025-26 exceeded market expectations.
• The robust growth was primarily driven by strong performance in the manufacturing and services sectors.
• Government spending on infrastructure and increased private consumption also contributed significantly to the positive trend.
• This growth trajectory signals economic resilience amidst global headwinds and reinforces India's position as a major growth engine.
• Economists highlight the need for sustained policy support to maintain momentum and address potential inflationary pressures.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth

articles.imageDescription: Graph depicting India's GDP growth with upward arrows.
articles.trivia: India's fiscal year runs from April 1 to March 31 of the following year.

articles.headline: 'Poshan 2.0' Phase II Launched to Combat Malnutrition and Enhance Nutritional Outcomes
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Women and Child Development launched Phase II of the 'Poshan 2.0' mission.
• This phase aims to intensify efforts to combat malnutrition, particularly among women and children, through an integrated approach.
• Key focus areas include strengthening Anganwadi services, promoting dietary diversity, and improving access to health and nutrition services.
• The mission emphasizes convergence with other government schemes like Ayushman Bharat and Swachh Bharat for holistic impact.
• Poshan 2.0 seeks to achieve specific targets for reducing stunting, wasting, anaemia, and low birth weight by 2026.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections

articles.headline: India-Nepal Border Coordination Meeting Addresses Cross-Border Crime and Security
articles.explainer:
• A high-level India-Nepal Border Coordination Meeting was held to discuss strategies for combating cross-border crime.
• Officials from both nations addressed issues such as human trafficking, drug smuggling, arms proliferation, and illegal activities along the open border.
• The meeting emphasized strengthening intelligence sharing, joint patrols, and coordinated operations between border guarding forces.
• Both sides reaffirmed their commitment to maintaining peace and stability along the border while ensuring legitimate movement of people and goods.
• This bilateral engagement is crucial for managing the unique challenges posed by the open border between India and Nepal.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas; linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security

Focus on combating cross-border crime like human trafficking and drug smuggling.
Emphasized intelligence sharing and joint patrols.

Evaluate the effectiveness of bilateral coordination mechanisms in addressing security threats and maintaining peace in border areas.
Suggest measures to enhance border infrastructure, surveillance capabilities, and community engagement to curb illegal activities.
articles.imageDescription: Map showing the India-Nepal border with security personnel patrolling.
articles.trivia: The Sashastra Seema Bal (SSB) is India's primary border guarding force for the Nepal and Bhutan borders.

articles.headline: Three New Wetland Sites Granted Conservation Status, Boosting Biodiversity Efforts
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change announced the designation of three new wetland sites with conservation status.
• These sites, located in different geographical regions, are recognized for their unique ecological importance and rich biodiversity.
• The designation aims to protect these critical ecosystems, which serve as habitats for migratory birds and support local livelihoods.
• Conservation efforts will focus on sustainable management, restoration of degraded areas, and community participation.
• This move aligns with India's commitments under the Ramsar Convention and its broader biodiversity conservation goals.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Environmental impact assessment
Disaster and disaster management
prelimsPoints: Three new wetland sites granted conservation status by the Ministry of Environment.

mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of wetlands and the threats they face in India.
Analyze the role of the Ramsar Convention and national policies in promoting wetland conservation and wise use.
Evaluate the challenges in managing wetland ecosystems, including encroachment, pollution, and climate change impacts.
Suggest community-based conservation models and sustainable livelihood options to ensure the long-term protection of wetlands.
articles.imageDescription: Scenic view of a wetland with migratory birds.
articles.trivia: The Ramsar Convention on Wetlands, signed in Ramsar, Iran, in 1971, is an intergovernmental treaty that provides the framework for the conservation and wise use of wetlands and their resources.

articles.headline: Government Releases Progress Report on National Quantum Mission, Highlights Key Advances
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Science & Technology released a progress report on India's National Quantum Mission (NQM).
• The report highlighted significant advancements in quantum computing, quantum communication, and quantum sensing technologies.
• Key achievements include the development of indigenous quantum processors and secure quantum communication networks.
• NQM aims to nurture a robust quantum ecosystem, attracting talent and fostering collaboration between academia and industry.
• This mission is crucial for India to become a global leader in quantum technologies, with applications in defense, finance, and healthcare.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights

Examine the challenges in developing quantum technologies, including talent acquisition, funding, and international collaboration.
articles.imageDescription: Abstract representation of quantum entanglement and quantum bits (qubits).
articles.trivia: A qubit (quantum bit) is the basic unit of quantum information, analogous to the bit in classical computing, but capable of existing in multiple states simultaneously due to superposition.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Reforms in Judicial Appointment Process to Enhance Transparency
articles.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee submitted its report recommending significant reforms in the judicial appointment process.
• The committee advocated for greater transparency and accountability in the appointment of judges to higher courts.
• Recommendations include establishing a transparent selection criteria, wider consultation, and a clear role for the executive.
• The report also suggested revisiting the Memorandum of Procedure (MoP) for judicial appointments to address existing bottlenecks.
• These recommendations aim to balance judicial independence with public accountability and streamline the appointment system.
gsSubTopics: Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary Ministries and Departments of the Government; pressure groups and formal/informal associations and their role in the Polity
Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies

Evaluate the challenges in balancing the executive's role and judicial autonomy in the appointment process of judges to higher courts.
Suggest a comprehensive framework for judicial appointments that addresses concerns of all stakeholders while upholding the integrity of the judiciary.
articles.imageDescription: Image of the Supreme Court of India building.
articles.trivia: The Collegium system, which evolved through Supreme Court judgments, is a system of appointment and transfer of judges that has no mention in the Indian Constitution.

articles.headline: PLI Scheme Expanded to Green Hydrogen and Advanced Materials Sectors
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved the expansion of the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme to include Green Hydrogen and Advanced Materials sectors.
• This move aims to boost domestic manufacturing, attract foreign investment, and create employment opportunities in these sunrise sectors.
• The PLI scheme provides incentives to companies for incremental sales from products manufactured in India.
• For Green Hydrogen, the scheme targets boosting its production and adoption, vital for India's energy transition goals.
• The expansion underscores the government's commitment to self-reliance (Atmanirbhar Bharat) and fostering innovation in strategic industries.

articles.imageDescription: Illustration of green hydrogen production facility and advanced material structures.
articles.trivia: The National Green Hydrogen Mission, approved in 2023, aims to make India a global hub for the production, utilization, and export of Green Hydrogen.

articles.headline: India Reiterates Call for Permanent Seat in UN Security Council at General Assembly Session
articles.explainer:
• India's representative at the UN General Assembly session reiterated its long-standing demand for a permanent seat in the UN Security Council (UNSC).
• The statement emphasized the need for comprehensive UNSC reforms to reflect contemporary global realities and enhance its legitimacy.
• India highlighted its contributions to global peace, security, and development as justifications for its inclusion as a permanent member.
• The call for reform is supported by the G4 nations (India, Germany, Japan, Brazil), advocating for expansion in both permanent and non-permanent categories.
• This renewed push underscores India's growing stature on the international stage and its aspiration for a greater role in global governance.

articles.imageDescription: Image of the United Nations headquarters building in New York.
articles.trivia: The five permanent members of the UN Security Council (P5) are China, France, Russia, the United Kingdom, and the United States.

articles.headline: National Skill Development Mission Targets 5 Million Youth by 2026 with Industry-Relevant Skills
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship announced an ambitious target under the National Skill Development Mission.
• The mission aims to skill and upskill 5 million youth by 2026, focusing on industry-relevant and future-ready skills.
• Key initiatives include expanding the reach of Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY) and strengthening ITIs.
• Emphasis is placed on digital skills, green skills, and emerging technologies to meet the demands of a changing job market.
• This initiative is crucial for enhancing employability, fostering entrepreneurship, and realizing India's demographic dividend.

articles.headline: Eminent Historian Rajat Kanta Ray Passes Away at 79
articles.explainer:
• Renowned Indian historian, Professor Rajat Kanta Ray, passed away at the age of 79.
• Ray was known for his extensive work on modern Indian history, particularly on nationalism, colonial encounters, and the Bengal Renaissance.
• He served as the Vice-Chancellor of Visva-Bharati University and held distinguished positions at various academic institutions.
• His scholarly contributions included critical analyses of Subaltern Studies and profound insights into socio-political movements.
• His demise marks a significant loss to the academic community and the field of historical research in India.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times

articles.imageDescription: Portrait of Professor Rajat Kanta Ray.
articles.trivia: Visva-Bharati University, founded by Rabindranath Tagore, is an institution of national importance located in Santiniketan, West Bengal.

articles.headline: Noted Assamese Dramatist and Lyricist Hemanta Dutta Dies at 83
articles.explainer:
• Veteran Assamese dramatist, lyricist, and cultural personality Hemanta Dutta passed away at the age of 83.
• He was a prolific writer, contributing significantly to Assamese theatre, music, and literature for decades.
• Dutta was celebrated for his poignant plays and soulful lyrics that often reflected the socio-cultural fabric of Assam.
• His works enriched the cultural landscape of the region and earned him several accolades.
• His demise is a great loss to the Assamese cultural fraternity and the broader Indian performing arts community.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times

His works reflected the socio-cultural fabric of Assam.
articles.imageDescription: Portrait of Hemanta Dutta.
articles.trivia: Assamese theatre has a rich history, with 'Bhaona', a traditional form of entertainment, being one of its earliest manifestations, introduced by Srimanta Sankardeva.

articles.headline: Veteran Actress Nazima, Known for Supporting Roles, Dies at 77
articles.explainer:

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the recent US tariffs imposed on Indian exports, consider the following statements:
1. The tariffs are set at 50% and are effective from August 27, 2025.
2. India's Ministry of Commerce has indicated that these tariffs are in line with WTO principles.
3. The move is expected to primarily impact India's agricultural exports to the US.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: US President Donald Trump announced a 50% tariff on certain Indian exports, effective August 27, 2025. Statement 2 is incorrect: India's Ministry of Commerce expressed strong disappointment, stating the tariffs violate WTO principles. Statement 3 is incorrect: The tariffs target specific sectors, not exclusively agriculture, where the US alleges unfair trade practices; the specific sectors are not mentioned in the source but it's not limited to agriculture.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Election Commission of India (ECI):
1. The ECI is a statutory body responsible for conducting elections in India.
2. Rahul Gandhi recently alleged that the ECI is involved in systemic voter list fraud.
3. The ECI is responsible for preparing and periodically revising electoral rolls.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: The ECI is a constitutional body (Article 324), not a statutory body. Statement 2 is correct: Rahul Gandhi alleged systemic voter list fraud by the ECI. Statement 3 is correct: The ECI is indeed responsible for the preparation and revision of electoral rolls.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following statements correctly describes the 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance adopted by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI)?
options: (a) It implies the RBI will inject more liquidity into the financial system to boost growth.
(b) It means the RBI is focused on ensuring inflation aligns with its target by gradually reducing liquidity.
(c) It refers to the RBI's policy of reducing interest rates to stimulate borrowing and investment.
(d) It indicates the RBI will maintain a neutral stance, neither tightening nor easing monetary policy.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) It means the RBI is focused on ensuring inflation aligns with its target by gradually reducing liquidity.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance signifies that the RBI is reducing the amount of money supply in the economy to control inflation. This is done by gradually increasing interest rates or reducing liquidity through various tools, thereby making borrowing more expensive and discouraging excessive spending, to bring inflation within the target.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to India's National Monsoon Action Plan 2025, which of the following are its key components?
1. Advanced weather forecasting and early warning systems.
2. Promotion of climate-resilient crop varieties.
3. Strengthening of irrigation infrastructure.
4. Compulsory crop insurance for all farmers.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statements 1, 2, and 3 are correct as per the explainer: The plan includes advanced weather forecasting, crop diversification, and improved irrigation infrastructure. Statement 4 is not explicitly mentioned as a component of the 'National Monsoon Action Plan 2025' in the provided text, although crop insurance is a general government initiative.

prelimsMocks.question: The Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023, recently upheld by the Supreme Court, primarily aims to:
options: (a) Regulate the use of digital currency and cryptocurrencies in India.
(b) Promote digital literacy and internet access across rural areas.
(c) Regulate the processing of digital personal data while recognizing individual privacy rights.
(d) Establish a national framework for cybersecurity and cybercrime prevention.
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) Regulate the processing of digital personal data while recognizing individual privacy rights.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The explainer clearly states: 'The DPDP Act aims to regulate the processing of digital personal data in a manner that recognizes both the right of individuals to protect their personal data and the need to process personal data for lawful purposes.' Options (a), (b), and (d) relate to other aspects of the digital landscape but are not the primary aim of the DPDP Act.

mainsMocks.question: The imposition of tariffs by major economies often signals a shift towards protectionism, impacting global trade and bilateral relations. mainsMocks.question: Electoral integrity is the bedrock of any robust democracy. In light of the recent allegations of systemic voter list fraud, discuss the role and independence of the Election Commission of India (ECI) and suggest reforms to enhance transparency and accuracy in electoral rolls. mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act; Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by emphasizing the importance of electoral integrity. Detail the constitutional mandate and functions of the ECI, highlighting its role as an independent body. Address the challenges to its independence and the specific allegations raised. Propose reforms for voter list accuracy (e.g., technology, robust grievance redressal, public scrutiny) and broader electoral transparency.

mainsMocks.question: India's space program, spearheaded by ISRO, has been a significant driver of national development. Evaluate the contributions of Earth Observation Satellites like EOS-08 in addressing India's socio-economic and environmental challenges, and discuss the future prospects of India's remote sensing capabilities. (250 words, 15 marks)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology; Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
mainsMocks.approach: Start with ISRO's role and the importance of earth observation. Detail specific applications of EOS-08 (agriculture, disaster management, urban planning, environmental monitoring). Discuss how these contribute to socio-economic development (food security, resource management) and environmental challenges (climate change, pollution). Conclude with India's increasing self-reliance and global leadership in remote sensing, outlining future prospects like advanced sensors, data analytics, and international collaboration.

• He served as the Vice-Chancellor of Visva-Bharati University and held distinguished positions at various academic institutions.
• His scholarly contributions included critical analyses of Subaltern Studies and profound insights into socio-political movements.
• His demise marks a significant loss to the academic community and the field of historical research in India.

• The ruling came on a batch of petitions challenging the Act on grounds of fundamental rights violations, particularly privacy and freedom of speech.
• The court emphasized the importance of balancing individual privacy rights with national security and legitimate state interests.
• This judgment provides clarity on data governance in India and sets precedents for future digital regulations.
• The DPDP Act aims to regulate the processing of digital personal data in a manner that recognizes both the right of individuals to protect their personal data and the need to process personal data for lawful purposes.

GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship announced an ambitious target under the National Skill Development Mission.
• The mission aims to skill and upskill 5 million youth by 2026, focusing on industry-relevant and future-ready skills.
• Key initiatives include expanding the reach of Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY) and strengthening ITIs.
• Emphasis is placed on digital skills, green skills, and emerging technologies to meet the demands of a changing job market.
• This initiative is crucial for enhancing employability, fostering entrepreneurship, and realizing India's demographic dividend.

GS2.trivia: The National Skill Development Mission was launched in 2015 to create convergence across various skill training schemes and departments.

GS2.headline: Former Governor of Nagaland, La. Ganesan, Passes Away at 80
GS2.explainer:
• La. Ganesan, a veteran politician and former Governor of Nagaland, passed away at the age of 80.
• He had a long and distinguished career in public service, primarily associated with the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP).
• Ganesan served as Governor of Nagaland from 2021 to 2024 and also held the gubernatorial post in Manipur and West Bengal (additional charge).
• Known for his administrative acumen and political experience, he contributed significantly to the states he served.
• His demise is a loss to Indian politics, particularly in the northeastern region.
gsSubTopics: Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies
prelimsPoints: La. Ganesan, former Governor of Nagaland, passed away at 80.
He served as Governor of Nagaland (2021-2024), Manipur, and West Bengal (additional charge).
Governors are constitutional heads of states, appointed by the President.
GS2.trivia: The Governor of a state is appointed by the President of India and holds office during the pleasure of the President, serving as a nominal head of the state executive.

GS2.headline: Jharkhand MLA Ramdas Soren Passes Away at 62
GS2.explainer:
• Ramdas Soren, a sitting Member of Legislative Assembly (MLA) from Jharkhand, passed away at the age of 62.
• He represented a constituency in Jharkhand and had a notable political career, serving two terms (2009–2014 and since 2019).
• Soren was a prominent voice for tribal rights and development in the state assembly.
• His demise creates a vacuum in regional politics and necessitates a by-election for his constituency.
• He was respected across party lines for his commitment to public service and welfare issues.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these

His demise will necessitate a by-election in his constituency.
MLAs are elected representatives in State Legislative Assemblies.
GS2.trivia: A by-election (or bye-election) is an election held to fill a vacancy arising during the term of a political office, typically when the incumbent dies or resigns.

GS3.headline: Three CRPF Personnel Killed, Ten Injured in Udhampur Gorge Plunge
GS3.explainer:
• Three Central Reserve Police Force (CRPF) personnel were killed in an accident in Udhampur, Jammu and Kashmir.
• Their vehicle reportedly plunged into a gorge in the Basantgarh area, causing multiple casualties.
• Ten other personnel sustained injuries and were immediately rushed to a nearby hospital for treatment.
• Authorities have initiated an investigation into the cause of the accident, citing difficult terrain conditions.
• The incident highlights the challenging operational environment faced by security forces in mountainous regions.

Highlight the sacrifices made by security personnel in maintaining internal security and territorial integrity.
GS3.imageDescription: Image of a CRPF vehicle on a mountainous road in Jammu and Kashmir.
GS3.trivia: The CRPF is one of the largest paramilitary forces in the world, primarily responsible for internal security, counter-insurgency, and law and order duties.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5%, Focuses on Inflation Management Amid Global Uncertainties
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to maintain the repo rate at 6.5%.
• This decision aligns with the RBI's ongoing commitment to withdraw accommodation to ensure inflation aligns with the target.
• Governor Shaktikanta Das emphasized vigilance against persistent core inflation and global economic uncertainties.
• The MPC also reaffirmed its stance of 'withdrawal of accommodation' to bring inflation within the 4% target.
• Analysts suggest the RBI is balancing economic growth support with the imperative of price stability.

mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control and economic growth in the current global economic scenario.

Examine the factors contributing to core inflation and the strategies the RBI employs to address them.
GS3.imageDescription: Image of the RBI logo with currency notes in the background.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in 2016 and is mandated to meet at least four times a year.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Earth Observation Satellite EOS-08
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched its advanced Earth Observation Satellite (EOS-08).
• The satellite was launched aboard a PSLV-C58 rocket from the Satish Dhawan Space Centre, Sriharikota.
• EOS-08 is designed to provide high-resolution imagery for various applications including agriculture, forestry, disaster management, and urban planning.
• This mission further strengthens India's capabilities in remote sensing and enhances its satellite constellation for environmental monitoring.
• The successful launch underscores ISRO's consistent progress in space technology and its contributions to national development.

GS3.imageDescription: Graphic illustration of a PSLV rocket launching an Earth Observation Satellite into orbit.
GS3.trivia: PSLV (Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle) is ISRO's third-generation launch vehicle, known for its reliability and versatility.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils New National Monsoon Action Plan to Enhance Agricultural Resilience
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare launched a comprehensive 'National Monsoon Action Plan 2025'.
• The plan aims to enhance agricultural resilience against erratic monsoon patterns and climate change impacts.
• Key components include advanced weather forecasting, crop diversification, improved irrigation infrastructure, and farmer awareness programs.
• Focus areas also include drought and flood preparedness, promoting climate-resilient crop varieties, and providing timely advisories.
• This initiative seeks to safeguard food security and stabilize rural incomes amidst changing climatic conditions.

GS3.imageDescription: Farmers working in a field with monsoon clouds in the background.
GS3.trivia: India's economy is often described as 'monsoon-dependent' due to the significant impact of rainfall on agricultural output and rural livelihoods.

GS3.headline: India's Q1 FY26 GDP Growth Exceeds Projections, Driven by Manufacturing and Services
GS3.explainer:
• India's Gross Domestic Product (GDP) growth for the first quarter (April-June) of FY2025-26 exceeded market expectations.
• The robust growth was primarily driven by strong performance in the manufacturing and services sectors.
• Government spending on infrastructure and increased private consumption also contributed significantly to the positive trend.
• This growth trajectory signals economic resilience amidst global headwinds and reinforces India's position as a major growth engine.
• Economists highlight the need for sustained policy support to maintain momentum and address potential inflationary pressures.

GS3.imageDescription: Graph depicting India's GDP growth with upward arrows.
GS3.trivia: India's fiscal year runs from April 1 to March 31 of the following year.

GS3.headline: India-Nepal Border Coordination Meeting Addresses Cross-Border Crime and Security
GS3.explainer:
• A high-level India-Nepal Border Coordination Meeting was held to discuss strategies for combating cross-border crime.
• Officials from both nations addressed issues such as human trafficking, drug smuggling, arms proliferation, and illegal activities along the open border.
• The meeting emphasized strengthening intelligence sharing, joint patrols, and coordinated operations between border guarding forces.
• Both sides reaffirmed their commitment to maintaining peace and stability along the border while ensuring legitimate movement of people and goods.
• This bilateral engagement is crucial for managing the unique challenges posed by the open border between India and Nepal.

GS3.imageDescription: Map showing the India-Nepal border with security personnel patrolling.
GS3.trivia: The Sashastra Seema Bal (SSB) is India's primary border guarding force for the Nepal and Bhutan borders.

GS3.headline: Three New Wetland Sites Granted Conservation Status, Boosting Biodiversity Efforts
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change announced the designation of three new wetland sites with conservation status.
• These sites, located in different geographical regions, are recognized for their unique ecological importance and rich biodiversity.
• The designation aims to protect these critical ecosystems, which serve as habitats for migratory birds and support local livelihoods.
• Conservation efforts will focus on sustainable management, restoration of degraded areas, and community participation.
• This move aligns with India's commitments under the Ramsar Convention and its broader biodiversity conservation goals.

Environmental impact assessment

GS3.imageDescription: Scenic view of a wetland with migratory birds.
GS3.trivia: The Ramsar Convention on Wetlands, signed in Ramsar, Iran, in 1971, is an intergovernmental treaty that provides the framework for the conservation and wise use of wetlands and their resources.

GS3.headline: Government Releases Progress Report on National Quantum Mission, Highlights Key Advances
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Science & Technology released a progress report on India's National Quantum Mission (NQM).
• The report highlighted significant advancements in quantum computing, quantum communication, and quantum sensing technologies.
• Key achievements include the development of indigenous quantum processors and secure quantum communication networks.
• NQM aims to nurture a robust quantum ecosystem, attracting talent and fostering collaboration between academia and industry.
• This mission is crucial for India to become a global leader in quantum technologies, with applications in defense, finance, and healthcare.

GS3.headline: PLI Scheme Expanded to Green Hydrogen and Advanced Materials Sectors
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved the expansion of the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme to include Green Hydrogen and Advanced Materials sectors.
• This move aims to boost domestic manufacturing, attract foreign investment, and create employment opportunities in these sunrise sectors.
• The PLI scheme provides incentives to companies for incremental sales from products manufactured in India.
• For Green Hydrogen, the scheme targets boosting its production and adoption, vital for India's energy transition goals.
• The expansion underscores the government's commitment to self-reliance (Atmanirbhar Bharat) and fostering innovation in strategic industries.
